reading_units.text: 天命之謂性，率性之謂道，脩道之謂教。

layers.translation_en: What Heaven has conferred is called The Nature; an accordance with this nature is called The Path of duty; the regulation of this path is called Instruction.

reading_units.text: 道也者，不可須臾離也，可離非道也。是故君子戒慎乎其所不睹，恐懼乎其所不聞。

layers.translation_en: The Way is something that may not be left for an instant; if it could be left, it would not be the Way. Therefore the superior man is watchful over himself when he does not see, and apprehensive when he does not hear.

reading_units.text: 莫見乎隱，莫顯乎微，故君子慎其獨也。

layers.translation_en: What is hidden should not be seen, and what is subtle should not be shown; therefore the noble person is ever watchful when alone.

reading_units.text: 喜怒哀樂之未發，謂之中；發而皆中節，謂之和。中也者，天下之大本也；和也者，天下之達道也。

layers.translation_en: The stillness of joy, anger, sorrow and pleasure is called equilibrium. When they arise and are each measured in moderation, this is called harmony. Equilibrium is the great root of all under heaven; harmony is the open Way of all under heaven.

reading_units.text: 致中和，天地位焉，萬物育焉。

layers.translation_en: When equilibrium and harmony are fully realized, heaven and earth assume their proper places and all things are nourished and flourish.

chapter.text:
天命之謂性，率性之謂道，脩道之謂教。
道也者，不可須臾離也，可離非道也。是故君子戒慎乎其所不睹，恐懼乎其所不聞。
莫見乎隱，莫顯乎微，故君子慎其獨也。
喜怒哀樂之未發，謂之中；發而皆中節，謂之和。中也者，天下之大本也；和也者，天下之達道也。
致中和，天地位焉，萬物育焉。

source_title: 四書章句集注 : 中庸章句
